content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (May 18, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on May 18, 2026, focused on cross-platform stability, deployment tooling, and benchmarking infrastructure. Key achievements include implementing Windows fallbacks for lifeops plugins, establishing the `elizaos deploy` CLI foundation, and introducing a side-by-side SWE-bench comparison harness. CI/CD pipelines were also enhanced with experimental ARM64 support and Flatpak offline-mode preparation.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Cross-Platform Stability and Packaging**
*   Implemented graceful Windows fallbacks for `plugin-lifeops` to prevent macOS-specific errors on non-darwin platforms ([#7788](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7788)).
*   Resolved circular symlink issues in the Docker pruner that caused health check failures in fresh `:develop` sandboxes ([#7783](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7783)).
*   Enabled experimental ARM64 support in the Snap CI matrix and prepared Flatpak manifests for offline-only build environments ([#7787](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7787)).

**Deployment and Benchmarking Infrastructure**
*   Established the foundation for the `elizaos deploy` CLI, providing a stable interface for creators to manage monetized app deployments ([#7786](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7786)).
*   Added a side-by-side comparison harness for SWE-bench Lite to enable head-to-head performance evaluation between elizaOS and opencode ([#7785](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7785)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   None.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   None.

## NEW ISSUES
*   None.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   None.
